goal: Stop the standalone outcome execution board runner from emitting duplicate distribution-architecture repairs for the same execution-board fingerprint during an unchanged review window.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.json: Codeberg movement is the primary success gate
marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now packet exists in the current review window
distribution_lane_latest.json: active lane remains distribution_architecture_repair until 2026-05-26T20:55:18
marketing_2026-05-26_192452_distribution_architecture_churn_guard_repair.json: same fingerprint already repaired in this window

changes: Added standalone runner reuse logic for distribution_architecture_repair / guard_follow_through / guard_pause based on existing fingerprint-matched execution logs.
Normalized timezone-aware historical timestamps before sorting recent marketing logs.
Extended reuse parsing to understand both outcome runner status payloads and run.py marketing-distribution-execution logs.

live_result.meaning: The standalone runner now reuses the current review-window architecture repair instead of minting another duplicate repair artifact for the same fingerprint.

result.summary: Patched the standalone execution-board runner so the hold-window follow-through path now fails closed on an unchanged fingerprint instead of producing another fake-progress architecture repair.
